subject: Statistics
title: Superforecasters and Bayesian Habits
description: A 60-second lesson on Superforecasters and Bayesian Habits.

hook.text: Philip Tetlock found that ordinary people can outpredict experts — if they think like Bayesians.

buildup.text: Superforecasters start with base rates, update incrementally, and never make extreme predictions.

discovery.text: They revise their estimates constantly. Each new piece of evidence nudges the probability by a small amount.

twist.text: Experts with deep knowledge but rigid beliefs performed worse than humble updaters with less expertise.

climax.text: The key traits: humility, granularity, and willingness to change your mind. Pure Bayesian virtues.

punchline.text: The best forecasters update constantly and believe nothing absolutely.

quiz.question: What makes superforecasters so accurate?
options: They start with base rates, update incrementally, and constantly revise their predictions
They have access to secret information
They refuse to change their initial predictions
